title: Today's Tech Brief: Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi
summary: A new autonomous vehicle hits the streets, Oura Ring 5 preorders open, and the tech world buzzes with innovation.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi
imageCaption: Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi
imageSourceTitle: Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your tech brief. Let's dive into the latest news. First up, Waymo's new robotaxi, Ojai, is set to hit the streets of Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Phoenix in the coming weeks. This Chinese-made autonomous vehicle is a significant move for Alphabet's self-driving arm, marking a new chapter in their expansion plans. I'm curious to see how Ojai will be received by the public, especially with its sleek design and advanced tech.

Ojai is Waymo's latest bid to dominate the autonomous ride-hailing space. With its advanced tech, it's sure to turn heads. But what really stands out is that it's made in China, highlighting the global nature of tech innovation. No word yet on when Ojai will be available to the general public, but I'm guessing it won't be long before we see these vehicles zipping around our cities. The implications for urban transportation are huge, and I'm excited to see how this technology evolves.

Here Comes Ojai, Waymo’s New Chinese-Made Robotaxi is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: There’s a new autonomous vehicle in town, or at least in the towns of Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Phoenix. Starting in the next few weeks, Alphabet self-driving vehicle developer Waymo will start picking up members of the public in its new Ojai vehicles (pronounced “oh hai”)—pale blue boxy minivans studded with sensors and complete with steering wheels,... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Here’s where you can preorder the new Oura Ring 5, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Gadgets Tech Verge Shopping Here’s where you can preorder the new Oura Ring 5 Oura’s slimmest smart ring launches on June 4th starting at $399, but it’s already up for preorder at multiple retailers. Oura’s slimmest smart ring launches on June 4th starting at $399, but it’s already up for preorder at multiple retailers. by Sheena Vasani May 28, 2026, 4:43 P... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
